metadata.purpose: Accessible, ADHD-friendly typography system. Prioritizes dyslexia-friendly letterforms + generous spacing.
metadata.rationale: ADHD users benefit from clear, open letterforms + high line-height. Body text minimum 16px on all devices. Display font (Inter) signals authority; body font (Poppins/Source Sans Pro) signals warmth. Mono (IBM Plex Mono) for clinical precision.

font_stack.recommendation: Use Inter + Poppins + IBM Plex Mono (Google Fonts or self-hosted). Fallback to system stack if loading fails.

display.name: Inter
display.why_chosen: Geometric sans-serif; highly legible; modern (signals clarity + authority). Designed for screens. Available free on Google Fonts.

characteristics.letterform_openness: Very open—prevents letter confusion (e.g., 0 vs O, l vs I)

display.accessibility_notes: No specific dyslexia features, but clean geometry reduces cognitive load. Good for visual hierarchy.

body.name: Poppins (preferred) or Source Sans Pro (alternative)
body.role: Body text, form labels, descriptions, paragraphs, helper text
body.why_chosen: Warm, open letterforms. Friendly personality (approachable). Excellent spacing between letters/words (reduces visual crowding). High x-height (easier to read).
body.dyslexia_friendly: Poppins & Source Sans Pro are in the 'accessibility-friendly' category. Not as specialized as OpenDyslexic, but widely tested as readable for dyslexic users.

mono.name: IBM Plex Mono
mono.role: Code, data values, timestamps, session IDs, technical labels
mono.why_chosen: Monospace clarity for clinical precision. Professional, not cute. Works at small sizes.

characteristics.letterform_clarity: Clear distinction between similar characters (1, l, I)

mono.usage_caution: Use only for data/code; not for body text (slower to read in para form).

2xs.usage: Helper text, captions, timestamps (use sparingly; low hierarchy)
2xs.note: Minimum for body-like content. Only for non-critical info.

xs.usage: Labels, secondary text, small UI copy
xs.note: Still readable at this size; use for form labels & helpers.

sm.usage: Body text, form input text, standard paragraph copy
sm.note: ADHD minimum. Never go smaller for body content.

base.usage: Primary body text, default size for most content

lg.usage: Larger body text (on desktop), intro paragraphs, emphasis
lg.note: Optional; use on desktop for visual breathing room.

xl.note: First heading level; clear hierarchy start.

2xl.note: Secondary hierarchy; breathing room around heading.

3xl.note: Primary visual hierarchy. Largest on page.

4xl.note: Only on desktop; may shrink to 30px on mobile.

tight.reasoning: Visual compactness for headlines; body text at this line-height is hard to read.

relaxed.usage: Body paragraphs, long-form content, assessment questions
relaxed.reasoning: ADHD-critical: generous spacing reduces visual crowding, eases reading. Research-backed for dyslexia-friendly.

light.usage: Not recommended; too thin for ADHD-friendly design

medium.usage: Form labels, button text, secondary emphasis
medium.reasoning: Slightly heavier; draws attention without being bold.

bold.usage: Primary headings (h1, h2), strong emphasis, section titles
bold.reasoning: Maximum visual weight; reserved for top hierarchy.

h1.usage: Page title, hero headline, main focus of section

h3.usage: Subsection, card title, question group heading

body_lg.usage: Large body paragraphs, emphasis paragraphs, intro text
body_lg.example: This assessment is not a diagnosis...

body.usage: Default body text, paragraphs, instructions, form content
body.example: Tell us about a time you struggled to stay organized at work.

label.usage: Form labels, input labels, field names
label.example: Have you had these symptoms in the last 6 months?

helper.usage: Helper text, explanations, form hints, secondary context
helper.example: This helps us understand your developmental history.

caption.usage: Image captions, timestamps, very secondary info

code.usage: Session IDs, technical values, data display

letter_spacing.note: Poppins & Source Sans Pro have good default spacing. No need for aggressive letter-spacing (can reduce readability). Only add relaxed spacing to headings if they feel cramped.

paragraph_spacing.reasoning: Generous spacing reduces cognitive load; prevents 'wall of text' feel.

dyslexia_friendly_features.open_letterforms: Poppins/Source Sans Pro have open 'a', 'e', 'g', 'y' shapes—reduces letter confusion
dyslexia_friendly_features.letter_spacing: Default + optional 0.2px in body text for extra clarity
dyslexia_friendly_features.x_height: Poppins/Source Sans Pro have high x-heights—easier to distinguish lowercase from uppercase

wcag_compliance.font_size_minimum: 16px for body text (WCAG AAA for readability)
wcag_compliance.contrast: All text on background: 7:1 minimum (WCAG AAA). Verified in palette.json.

cognitive_accessibility.sentence_length: Design recommends: max 20 words/sentence in assessment UI
cognitive_accessibility.paragraph_length: Max 3–4 sentences per paragraph (reduces cognitive overwhelm)
cognitive_accessibility.visual_hierarchy: Clear h1 > h2 > h3 > body progression (no skipped levels)

mobile_first.rationale: Mobile users: smaller screens, longer reading sessions. Generous sizes reduce strain.

tablet.rationale: Slightly larger headings; more breathing room.

desktop.line_height_increase: 1.75 (can increase to 1.8 on desktop for max readability)
desktop.rationale: Larger screens allow bigger headlines without feeling cramped; body text stays at 16px minimum.

implementation_notes.css_approach: CSS custom properties for type scale (--type-h1, --type-body, etc.)
implementation_notes.next_js_font_loading: Use next/font with @import for Inter, Poppins, IBM Plex Mono. Ensure font-display: swap to avoid CLS.
implementation_notes.tailwindcss_integration: Extend theme.fontSize with custom scale: { 'heading-1': '30px', 'heading-2': '24px', etc. }
implementation_notes.mui_integration: Override typography in MUI theme config: { typography: { h1: { fontFamily: 'Inter', fontSize: '30px', fontWeight: 700 }, body1: { fontFamily: 'Poppins', fontSize: '16px', lineHeight: 1.75 } } }
implementation_notes.fallback_stack: If Google Fonts CDN fails, system font stack ensures readability. Poppins fallback: Helvetica Neue, -apple-system, sans-serif.

testing_checklist: ✓ All body text ≥16px on all devices
✓ Body uses Poppins/Source Sans Pro (warm, accessible)
✓ Mono used only for data/code (not body text)
✓ Tested with dyslexia simulator (Poppins/Source Sans Pro pass)
✓ Contrast verified (7:1 on all background colors)
✓ Responsive type tested at mobile, tablet, desktop
✓ Font loading tested (Google Fonts CDN + system fallback)
